{"credits": [{"type": "finder", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/47360190?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/pranjalithakur", "id": 47360190, "login": "pranjalithakur", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjQ3MzYwMTkw", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/pranjalithakur", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-54466", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 9.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:H/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-130", "name": "Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency"}], "description": "### Impact

The frame format in draft versions of the WebSocket protocol includes a length header that allows an arbitrarily large integer to be encoded as a sequence of bytes with the high bit set. By sending an indefinite sequence of bytes with values `0x80` or above, a client can make the server parse these bytes into an ever-growing integer. Since JavaScript numbers are 64-bit floating point values, this number will eventually lose precision and lead to the subsequent payload being parsed incorrectly.

### Patches

The issue has been patched in version 0.7.5 by rejecting the message if the length header exceeds the configured maximum message length. All users should upgrade to this version.

### Workarounds

No known workarounds exist.

### Acknowledgements

This issue was discovered and reported by Pranjali Thakur, DepthFirst Security Research Team.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00445, "percentile": 0.36004}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-15T22:07:24Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-54466"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-15T22:07:24Z", "references": ["https://github.com/faye/websocket-driver-node/security/advisories/GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6", "https://github.com/faye/websocket-driver-node/releases/tag/0.7.5", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/faye/websocket-driver-node/security-advisories/GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/faye/websocket-driver-node", "summary": "websocket-driver: Message corruption via abuse of protocol length headers", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-15T22:07:25Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-xv26-6w52-cph6", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.7.5", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "websocket-driver"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 0.7.5"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: websocket-driver: Message corruption via abuse of protocol length headers
